title: Claude Code Agent Frontmatter
description: Schema for validating YAML frontmatter in agent .md files

name.description: Agent identifier (kebab-case). If omitted, the filename (minus .md) is used as the agent name.

description.description: When and why to use this agent - used for agent discovery.
examples: Performs thorough code review focusing on correctness, style, and potential bugs. Use when reviewing pull requests or auditing code quality.
Audits infrastructure and application code for security vulnerabilities. Use when reviewing authentication flows, input validation, or dependency exposure.

tools.description: Tools this agent is permitted to use. Accepts a YAML array or a comma-separated string of known built-in tool names or MCP tool patterns (mcp__<server>__<tool>).

oneOf.description: Comma-separated list of tool names (parsed from YAML scalar)

allowed-tools.description: Alias for 'tools' — tools this agent is permitted to use. Some agent files use this hyphenated form. Accepts a YAML array or a comma-separated string.

oneOf.description: Comma-separated list of tool names (parsed from YAML scalar)

model.description: The model to use when this agent is invoked. Use 'inherit' to use whatever model the parent context is running.

model_rationale.description: Explains why a specific model was chosen for this agent. Recommended whenever 'model' is set to a non-default value.
examples: Uses opus for deep reasoning over complex security vulnerability patterns
Uses haiku for fast, low-cost summarisation tasks that do not require advanced reasoning

color.description: Display color for the agent. Used in UI contexts that render agent identity.

user-invocable.description: Whether this agent can be directly invoked by the user. When false, the agent is only available for model-initiated delegation.

system_prompt.description: The system prompt that defines the agent's behavior, instructions, and persona. This is the core content of YAML-based agent definitions.

maxTurns.description: Maximum number of agentic turns (API round-trips) before the agent stops. Controls execution budget.

permissionMode.description: Controls how the agent handles permission prompts.

hooks.description: Hook configurations scoped to this agent's execution. Uses the same event names and handler schema as hooks.json.

examples.name: code-reviewer
examples.description: Performs thorough code review focusing on correctness, maintainability, and potential bugs. Use when reviewing pull requests, auditing code quality, or assessing a new codebase.
examples.model_rationale: Sonnet balances review quality and speed for typical code review tasks

examples.name: security-auditor
examples.description: Audits code and infrastructure for security vulnerabilities. Use when reviewing authentication flows, input validation, dependency exposure, or hardcoded secrets.
examples.model_rationale: Uses opus for deeper reasoning over subtle vulnerability patterns and complex attack surfaces

examples.name: doc-writer
examples.description: Generates and updates technical documentation from source code and comments. Use when producing API docs, README files, or inline documentation.
